# Customs and Duties

In the context of international shipping, for eligible destinations, import duties and taxes are pre-collected at checkout through a delivered-duty-paid arrangement, ensuring that the recipient is not billed upon arrival. The only exception to this rule is for oversized freight, where the destination customs office determines duties and bills them separately due to the difficulty in estimating the item's value beforehand.

[Source: international_shipping.md#customs-and-duties]
